page: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes all rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Passmark CPU scores, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests, also place in the top 10%. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the middle 50%, though Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores remain strong in the top 25%.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which accelerate vector and matrix operations. This hardware acceleration contributes to top-tier performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. For large language model (LLM) inference, prompt processing with a 70B model achieves top-tier speeds, while text generation with medium and large models ranks in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.metal-48xl offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making this bare-metal instance best suited for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, heavy enterprise applications, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

Operating at 3.2 GHz, the x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While the server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains in the middle 50%. The high memory density and specialized instruction sets make this server highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis caching, software compilation, and CPU-based large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring massive DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores at 3.2 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector operations
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and LLM prompt processing
